respiratoryOrders: Perform focused respiratory assessment as needed for dyspnea or cough. Monitor vital signs and report worsening symptoms to nurse. Frequency: as needed. Timing: During sleep period. Related to impaired airway clearance.

respiratoryOrders: Assist resident with flutter valve therapy as ordered. Instruct steady exhalation through device, rest between cycles, and cough after each set. Frequency: at bedtime. Timing: When awake. Related to shortness of breath.

Assist resident with home CPAP or BiPAP device. Fill humidifier with distilled water if applicable and notify nurse of refusal or device concerns. Frequency: every morning. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to atelectasis risk.

Complete respiratory assessment before and after intervention. Include breath sounds, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, cough quality, and resident tolerance. Frequency: every morning. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to impaired airway clearance.

respiratoryOrders: Assess breath sounds, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and work of breathing each shift. Notify nurse of new wheezing, crackles, or increased oxygen need. Frequency: at bedtime. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to ventilator dependence.

Suction tracheostomy as clinically indicated for audible secretions, visible secretions, or ineffective cough. Document secretion color, amount, and tolerance. Frequency: every shift. Timing: Evening shift. Related to impaired airway clearance.

Provide inhalation therapy as ordered and monitor for tremor, tachycardia, throat irritation, or increased shortness of breath. Frequency: every shift. Timing: Day shift. Related to retained secretions.

Ensure noninvasive ventilation device is functioning before sleep period. Monitor for mask leak, discomfort, dryness, or signs of respiratory distress. Frequency: three times daily. Timing: After activity. Related to ventilator dependence.

respiratoryOrders: Monitor ventilated resident for breath sounds, synchrony, secretions, oxygen saturation, and alarm events. Document findings and notify nurse of changes. Frequency: before and after therapy. Timing: During sleep period. Related to shortness of breath.

Evaluate resident during inhaler use to ensure proper hand-breath coordination. Document medication delivery, tolerance, and symptom response. Frequency: every shift. Timing: Before activity. Related to retained secretions.

Complete post-treatment respiratory assessment and document effectiveness of intervention, resident comfort, and any adverse response. Frequency: three times daily. Timing: After activity. Related to chronic respiratory failure.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 89%, RR 26 bpm. Treatment status: Assessment overdue.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 89%, HR 91 bpm. Symptoms noted: HR increased.

respiratoryOrders: Perform tracheal suctioning as needed for airway clearance per facility protocol. Use sterile technique and monitor oxygen saturation and distress. Frequency: every shift. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to post-hospital respiratory weakness.

Provide positive expiratory pressure airway clearance using facility-approved device. Clean equipment per protocol and document resident tolerance. Frequency: before and after therapy. Timing: When awake. Related to retained secretions.

Provide inhalation therapy as ordered and monitor for tremor, tachycardia, throat irritation, or increased shortness of breath. Frequency: during sleep. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to pneumonia recovery.

Provide oral suctioning as needed for excessive secretions using approved technique. Assess breath sounds and resident comfort before and after. Frequency: every morning. Timing: Day shift. Related to retained secretions.

Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory status each shift. Report saturation below ordered parameters, increased shortness of breath, or change in mentation. Frequency: every shift. Timing: Day shift. Related to obstructive sleep apnea.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 93%, RR 22 bpm. Treatment status: Assessment overdue.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 93%, HR 86 bpm. Symptoms noted: HR increased.

respiratoryOrders: Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory status each shift. Report saturation below ordered parameters, increased shortness of breath, or change in mentation. Frequency: every evening. Timing: Night shift. Related to obstructive sleep apnea.

Assist resident with home CPAP or BiPAP device. Fill humidifier with distilled water if applicable and notify nurse of refusal or device concerns. Frequency: twice daily. Timing: As clinically indicated. Related to obstructive sleep apnea.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 92%, RR 25 bpm. Treatment status: Completed.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 92%, HR 98 bpm. Symptoms noted: Labored breathing, Nasal flaring, Coughing.

respiratoryOrders: Evaluate resident during inhaler use to ensure proper hand-breath coordination. Document medication delivery, tolerance, and symptom response. Frequency: at bedtime. Timing: Evening shift. Related to atelectasis risk.

Evaluate resident for shortness of breath, wheezing, secretion burden, and therapy response. Document findings and communicate change in condition. Frequency: twice daily. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to impaired airway clearance.

Verify ventilator settings against provider order at start of shift. Check alarms, circuit connections, humidification, and resident tolerance. Frequency: every evening. Timing: Day shift. Related to retained secretions.

Respiratory staff or licensed nurse to evaluate tracheostomy status and airway patency. Notify provider for obstruction concerns or cuff issues. Frequency: daily. Timing: Evening shift. Related to ventilator dependence.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 93%, RR 22 bpm. Treatment status: Completed.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 93%, HR 91 bpm. Symptoms noted: Irregular RR, Rhonchi found.

respiratoryOrders: Provide biofeedback-assisted breathing instruction using approved facility equipment as clinically indicated. Document resident participation and tolerance. Frequency: daily. Timing: After activity. Related to chronic respiratory failure.

Assist resident with metered-dose inhaler and spacer technique. Instruct slow inhalation, breath hold as tolerated, and mouth rinse when indicated. Frequency: every morning. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to atelectasis risk.

Respiratory staff or licensed nurse to evaluate tracheostomy status and airway patency. Notify provider for obstruction concerns or cuff issues. Frequency: before and after therapy. Timing: Before activity. Related to atelectasis risk.

Coach resident through huff coughing after chest physiotherapy. Encourage hydration as permitted and document secretion amount and character if produced. Frequency: at bedtime. Timing: Evening shift. Related to shortness of breath.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 91%, RR 27 bpm. Treatment status: Assessment overdue.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 91%, HR 99 bpm. Symptoms noted: Coughing, Chest tightness, Labored breathing, HR increased.

respiratoryOrders: Complete post-treatment respiratory assessment and document effectiveness of intervention, resident comfort, and any adverse response. Frequency: daily. Timing: Day shift. Related to tracheostomy dependence.

Provide positive expiratory pressure airway clearance using facility-approved device. Clean equipment per protocol and document resident tolerance. Frequency: before and after therapy. Timing: During sleep period. Related to retained secretions.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 93%, RR 23 bpm. Treatment status: Completed.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 93%, HR 93 bpm. Symptoms noted: Chest tightness, Wheezing.

respiratoryOrders: Assist with Yankauer suction for oral secretions as needed. Stop for bleeding, distress, or persistent desaturation and notify nurse. Frequency: before and after therapy. Timing: Day shift. Related to pneumonia recovery.

Provide chest wall manipulation per care plan for airway clearance. Pause treatment for pain, dizziness, acute distress, or resident refusal. Frequency: during sleep. Timing: During sleep period. Related to shortness of breath.

notes.author: J. Huang
notes.body: Morning assessment. SpO2 93%, RR 17 bpm. Treatment status: Assessment overdue.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 93%, HR 72 bpm. No new symptoms.

respiratoryOrders: Respiratory staff to complete guided breathing exercises as tolerated. Monitor respiratory rate, work of breathing, and resident comfort before and after intervention. Frequency: daily. Timing: Evening shift. Related to ventilator dependence.

Apply BiPAP at bedtime and during naps as tolerated. Verify settings per provider order, check skin integrity around mask, and document hours used. Frequency: at bedtime. Timing: After activity. Related to shortness of breath.

Ensure ventilator tubing is secure and free of obstruction. Respond to alarms promptly and assess resident before silencing alarm. Frequency: twice daily. Timing: During sleep period. Related to shortness of breath.

Assess breath sounds, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and work of breathing each shift. Notify nurse of new wheezing, crackles, or increased oxygen need. Frequency: three times daily. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to COPD.

Provide positive expiratory pressure airway clearance using facility-approved device. Clean equipment per protocol and document resident tolerance. Frequency: every shift. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to tracheostomy dependence.

notes.author: L. Hernandez
notes.body: Evening vitals stable. SpO2 95%, HR 82 bpm. Symptoms noted: RR increased, Coughing.

respiratoryOrders: Assess breath sounds, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and work of breathing each shift. Notify nurse of new wheezing, crackles, or increased oxygen need. Frequency: at bedtime. Timing: With scheduled respiratory treatment. Related to ventilator dependence.

Suction tracheostomy as clinically indicated for audible secretions, visible secretions, or ineffective cough. Document secretion color, amount, and tolerance. Frequency: every shift. Timing: Evening shift. Related to impaired airway clearance.

Provide inhalation therapy as ordered and monitor for tremor, tachycardia, throat irritation, or increased shortness of breath. Frequency: every shift. Timing: Day shift. Related to retained secretions.

Ensure noninvasive ventilation device is functioning before sleep period. Monitor for mask leak, discomfort, dryness, or signs of respiratory distress. Frequency: three times daily. Timing: After activity. Related to ventilator dependence.

Administer nebulizer treatment per medication order. Assess breath sounds, respiratory rate, and oxygen saturation before and after treatment. Frequency: twice daily. Timing: Evening shift. Related to chronic respiratory failure.
